creator: delphij@delphij.net
text:
There is a race between fork(2) and [sg]etpriority where when a
newly born process in allproc list, [sg]etpriority could end up with a
NULL deference which would lead to a panic.

Fix: An easy fix for this would be to guard with PRS_NEW state
by just skipping it.  However, we are still looking for better
solution for the issue.
How-To-Repeat: 	The following shell script would demostrate the race and can easily
trigger panic with non-privileged user, on a multi-core system, in my case
Dell 2950:

--- demo.sh begins here ---
#!/bin/sh

P=0

while [ ${P} -lt 200 ];
do
        sleep 3 &
        renice +4 -u delphij &
        sleep 3 &
        renice +4 -u delphij &
        sleep 3 &
        renice +4 -u delphij &
        sleep 3 &
        renice +4 -u delphij &
        P=`expr ${P} + 1`
done
--- demo.sh ends here ---
